compiled_state.title: [review workflow]: shellcheck disable comment triggers full re-review -> 3 review passes instead of 1
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
Adding a single # shellcheck disable=SC2016 comment to fix a pre-commit hook failure invalidated the diff hash, requiring a full re-review cycle (light-tier review + record-review.sh) for a change that was purely an annotation. This is the second time this session where a non-semantic change (comment/annotation) after a passing review required a full review re-run.

## Expected Behavior
Changes that are exclusively annotations, comments, or suppression directives with no semantic impact should be exemptible from full re-review (e.g., via a fast-path that re-records the existing review findings against the new hash).

## Actual Behavior
Any file change after review passes invalidates the diff hash unconditionally, even for comment-only edits, requiring full reviewer agent dispatch.

comments.body: Triaged (debug-everything): LLM-behavioral bug (skill/agent/prompt). Requires bot-psychologist investigation and user approval. Deferred to end-of-session user review.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: INTENT_AMBIGUOUS (2026-04-20): Intent-search verdict — diff-hash invalidation on any change may be deliberate design (review gate integrity). Ticket proposes a comment-only fast-path exemption. Requires user decision: is this a bug (review cycle waste) or intended behavior (integrity gate)? Deferred pending user input.
comments.author: Test
